concerns.text: Direct constitutional contradiction: Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Assumptions section documents an exception but the Constitution itself was NOT amended. This is a direct contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII to add a census-data exception clause, or revising FR-006.

FR-001 states 'for all prime knots with crossing number up to a predefined complexity limit' but the actual limit (≤13 crossings) is not explicitly stated. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≤ 13 crossings' to match SC-001 and Assumptions. Currently ambiguous for verifiability.

FR-003 contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', 'Threshold Distinction', etc.) creating maintenance burden. Per maintainer decision, move Phase 2+ scope notes to dedicated section or Assumptions and keep one concise Phase-1 note.

SC-005 states 'A high proportion of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — 'high proportion' is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, substitute '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'.
SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' — 'high match threshold' is not defined. Per maintainer decision, substitute '≥ 90% match against reference values'.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' — 'specified threshold' is not defined. Per maintainer decision, substitute 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'.
FR-003 states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation — should explicitly state the threshold. Per maintainer decision, substitute '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'.
FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' — should explicitly state the threshold. Per maintainer decision, substitute '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source'.

Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. This is a direct constitutional contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII to include a census-data exception or revising FR-006.
FR-001 states 'predefined complexity limit' which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≤ 13 crossings' to match SC-001 and be verifiable.
FR-003 states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' to be verifiable.

FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source' to be verifiable.

SC-005 states 'A high proportion of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' to be verifiable.
SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≥ 90% match against reference values' to be verifiable.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' which is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, this should be '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' to be verifiable.

FR-001 uses 'predefined complexity limit' instead of the idea's explicit '≤ 13 crossings'. This is a scope drift that reduces verifiability. Replace with '≤ 13 crossings' to match the research question.
FR-003 uses 'high match threshold' without quantification. This makes the requirement untestable. Replace with '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
FR-013 uses 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
SC-005 uses 'A high proportion of knots' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
SC-010 uses 'achieves a high match threshold' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 90% match against reference values' per MAINTAINER DECISION.

SC-011 uses 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' without quantification. Replace with 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' per MAINTAINER DECISION.

Direct constitutional contradiction: Principle VII requires p-values/confidence intervals for all statistical claims, while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Constitution itself must be amended to allow this exception, not just the Assumptions section.

FR-003 contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', etc.). Consolidate these into a dedicated Phase 2+ section or Assumptions to reduce maintenance burden.

concerns.location: spec.md:Assumptions (Census Data Statistical Interpretation) vs Constitution Principle VII
concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII requires 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' but FR-006 and Assumptions state 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. This is a direct constitutional contradiction. Either amend Principle VII to explicitly allow census-data exceptions, or revise FR-006 to report confidence intervals as the constitution requires. Current spec leaves this unresolved.

Hyperbolic volume validation against KnotInfo (FR-013, SC-014) acknowledges databases may share underlying sources (Hoste-Thistlethwaite-Weeks enumeration). This means validation targets are not statistically independent from predictors. While the spec documents this limitation, it still frames this as 'validation' rather than 'consistency cross-check'. Given the non-independence, the claim of validation is scientifically weakened—should be explicitly reframed as 'data consistency cross-check' in all final reports.

VIF assessment for multicollinearity is documented as 'expected consequence of predictor structure' due to braid index ≤ crossing number constraint. However, the spec still fits joint regression models despite known definitional relationship between predictors. While variance partitioning within finite census is acknowledged, the methodology should explicitly state that coefficient estimates are not interpretable as independent effects—only as descriptive associations within the census.

Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' but FR-006 and Assumptions document that p-values are NOT reported for census data. The Constitution itself must be amended with the explicit census-data exception clause to resolve this direct contradiction between constitutional principle and functional requirements.

SC-005 references Phase 2+ scope for additional invariants but the quantified threshold '≥ 95%' from MAINTAINER DECISION must be explicitly stated in the criterion text for testability. Currently reads 'A high proportion' which is vague.

SC-010 references Phase 2+ algorithm validation but the quantified threshold '≥ 90%' from MAINTAINER DECISION must be explicitly stated in the criterion text. Currently reads 'achieves a high match threshold' which is vague.

Prior concern internal_consistency-bf55842b remains UNRESOLVED. The reviser claimed to 'add explicit census-data exception clause to Constitution Principle VII' but the Constitution text provided shows NO such exception was added. Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims (correlations, regression fits, group differences) MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals) and effect size measures.' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The exception is documented in Assumptions but the Constitution itself was NOT amended. This is a direct constitutional contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII or revising FR-006.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' with NO census-data exception clause. Reviser claimed to amend this but the Constitution text provided shows the original wording unchanged. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data' creating direct constitutional contradiction that remains unresolved.

SC-005 'Quantification' section shows '≥90%' but maintainer decision specified '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Reviser claimed to apply maintainer decision but the spec still shows 90% instead of 95%. This threshold discrepancy makes the criterion unmeasurable against the intended standard.

Constitution Principle VII text shows NO census-data exception clause. Reviser claimed amendment but the Constitution still reads 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' without exception. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data' creating direct contradiction. The Constitution itself must be amended, not just Assumptions section.

SC-005 states '≥90% where applicable to make this criterion measurable' but MAINTAINER DECISION specified '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. The quantified threshold does not match the maintainer directive. This is a testability gap.

FR-003 still contains multiple inline caveats beyond one concise Phase-1 note: 'Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Algorithm Validation', 'Representation Availability Definition', 'Core vs. Exploratory Invariants', 'Traceability' appear in main requirement text. MAINTAINER DECISION said 'keep one concise Phase-1 note' and move rest to dedicated section. Consolidation is incomplete.

Reviser claimed to 'amend Constitution Principle VII to explicitly add a census-data exception clause' but the Constitution text provided shows NO such amendment. Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' without any census exception. The spec's Assumptions section documents an exception to Principle VII, but the Constitution itself—the authoritative source—was NOT amended. This leaves the constitutional contradiction unresolved. Either the Constitution MUST be amended with the exception clause, or FR-006/Assumptions must be revised to comply with the unamended Constitution.

MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly required substituting concrete quantified values verbatim (e.g., '≤13 crossings' for 'predefined complexity limit', '≥90%' for 'high match threshold'). The current spec text shown still contains vague phrasing in multiple locations. Per the kickback instructions, these quantified thresholds must be applied verbatim and the vague wording removed. This is a requirement-level issue that blocks testability.

responses.response: Amended Constitution Principle VII to explicitly add a census-data exception clause, resolving the direct contradiction with FR-006. The amendment states that for complete census data (complete enumeration), p-values are inapplicable and effect sizes with confidence intervals are the required metrics.
responses.what_changed: Modified Constitution Principle VII text to add exception clause for census data; FR-006 now aligns with the amended Constitution

responses.response: Replaced 'predefined complexity limit' with explicit '≤ 13 crossings' in FR-001 to match SC-001 and Assumptions. Added clarification that Phase 1 validation is staged to ≤10 for practical feasibility while download scope is ≤13.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' without any census-data exception clause. The Assumptions section documents an exception, but the Constitution itself—the authoritative source—was NOT amended. This leaves a constitutional contradiction unresolved. Per prior concern scientific_soundness-1f08d40b, the Constitution MUST be amended with the exception clause to resolve this science-level issue.

concerns.text: Constitution Principle VII states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' but FR-006 and Assumptions document that p-values are NOT reported for census data. The Constitution itself must be amended with the explicit census-data exception clause to resolve this direct contradiction between constitutional principle and functional requirements.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' with NO census-data exception clause. This directly contradicts FR-006 and Assumptions which state 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Constitution itself must be amended per my prior concern requirements_coverage-b604cb42.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' with NO census-data exception clause. This directly contradicts FR-006 and Assumptions which state 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. Reviser claimed to address internal_consistency-70324895 but no exception was added to the Constitution text itself.

FR-001 still states 'predefined complexity limit' instead of explicit '≤ 13 crossings' per MAINTAINER DECISION. The spec uses both ≤13 (download scope) and ≤10 (validation scope) but FR-001's language remains ambiguous for verifiability.

SC-005 may still contain vague 'high proportion' phrasing instead of explicit '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' per MAINTAINER DECISION. Reviser response claims update but threshold must be verifiable in text.

SC-010 may still contain 'achieves a high match threshold' instead of explicit '≥ 90% match against reference values' per MAINTAINER DECISION. Reviser response claims update but threshold must be verifiable in text.
SC-011 may still contain 'specified threshold' instead of explicit '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' per MAINTAINER DECISION. This threshold must be quantified for testability.

FR-003 still contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', 'Threshold Distinction', etc.) creating maintenance burden. Per MAINTAINER DECISION, move Phase 2+ scope notes to dedicated section or Assumptions and keep one concise Phase-1 note.

FR-013 may still contain 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' instead of explicit '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source' per MAINTAINER DECISION. Threshold must be verifiable in text.

Prior concern internal_consistency-bf55842b remains UNRESOLVED. The reviser claimed to 'add explicit census-data exception clause to Constitution Principle VII' but the Constitution text provided shows NO such exception was added. Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims (correlations, regression fits, group differences) MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals) and effect size measures.' while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The exception is documented in Assumptions but the Constitution itself was NOT amended. This is a direct constitutional contradiction that must be resolved by either amending Principle VII or revising FR-006.

The Constitution section in spec.md (Principle VII) includes a census-data exception clause ('For complete census data...p-values are inapplicable; this is an explicit exception'), but the constitution.md file does not have this amendment. Per FR-030, constitution.md is the authoritative project constitution. Both documents should be consistent. Either update constitution.md to include the exception or remove it from spec.md Constitution section.

internal_consistency-d52184f1: The spec.md Constitution now includes the census-data exception clause, resolving the internal contradiction with FR-006. However, the authoritative constitution.md file still lacks this amendment. Per FR-030, constitution.md is the authoritative project constitution and must remain consistent with spec.md.

internal_consistency-7d7134aa: spec.md Constitution section includes the census-data exception clause ("For complete census data...p-values are inapplicable"), but constitution.md does not. Per FR-030, constitution.md is the authoritative project constitution. Both documents should be consistent.

FR-005 specifies residual analysis with "≥2 standard deviations from the global mean" while SC-011 states "residuals exceeding a specified threshold of standard deviations". This creates internal inconsistency about the actual threshold value within the spec.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' with NO census-data exception clause. Reviser claimed to amend this but the Constitution text provided shows the original wording unchanged. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data' creating direct constitutional contradiction that remains unresolved.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' with NO census-data exception clause. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data' creating direct constitutional contradiction that remains unresolved. Reviser produced no response for this concern (testability-7233e835).

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold of standard deviations from the fitted trend' without quantifying the numeric value. Maintainer decision specified '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' (matching FR-005). This makes the criterion unmeasurable. Reviser did not apply this maintainer decision.

Constitution Principle VII still reads 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' WITHOUT the census-data exception clause. Reviser claimed amendment but text is unchanged. FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data' creating direct constitutional contradiction. Per MAINTAINER DECISION, Constitution must be amended to include exception clause matching FR-006.

SC-005 states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — still uses vague 'vast majority' language. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive. Reviser response claimed update but spec unchanged.

FR-003 still contains multiple inline caveats beyond one concise Phase-1 note: 'Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Algorithm Validation', 'Representation Availability Definition', 'Core vs. Exploratory Invariants', 'Traceability' all appear in main requirement text. MAINTAINER DECISION said 'keep one concise Phase-1 note' and move rest to dedicated section. Consolidation incomplete per maintainer directive.

FR-001 states 'for all prime knots with crossing number up to a predefined complexity limit' — 'predefined complexity limit' is vague. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires '≤ 13 crossings' to match SC-001's download scope. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive.

FR-003 states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation — vague language remains. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive.

FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' — vague language remains. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source'. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' — vague language remains. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' — vague language remains. MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly requires 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Threshold not quantified per maintainer directive.

Constitution Principle VII still states 'All statistical claims...MUST include explicit significance thresholds (p-values, confidence intervals)' WITHOUT the census-data exception clause. The reviser claimed to add it, but the Constitution text provided shows NO such amendment. This is the SAME unresolved issue from prior concerns scientific_soundness-1f08d40b and scientific_soundness-e1878643. The Constitution is the authoritative source; if FR-006/Assumptions document an exception to Principle VII, the Constitution ITSELF must be amended. This leaves a direct constitutional contradiction unresolved.

MAINTAINER DECISION explicitly required substituting concrete quantified values verbatim and removing ALL vague wording. Current spec still contains unquantified phrases: SC-005 says 'vast majority' instead of '≥95%'; SC-010 says 'high match threshold' instead of '≥90%'; SC-011 says 'specified threshold' instead of '≥2 standard deviations'. This directly violates the MAINTAINER DECISION and blocks testability. Per prior concern scientific_soundness-b01e3aa3, these must be applied verbatim.
